channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Hungary Election Results: Viktor Orban Concedes Defeat and Congratulates Peter Magyar
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has inspired populist movements globally, could not overcome the growing dissatisfaction of his own citizens.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:credit: Bernadett Szabo/Reuters
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban on Sunday. His defeat will please the European Union and is likely to disappoint President Trump.

item.title: Orban Loss May Ease Hungary’s Tensions with European Union
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has stymied the European Union for years, and particularly in recent months. Peter Magyar’s election could change that, if only so much.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.media:credit: Denes Erdos/Associated Press
item.media:description: Celebrations in Budapest on Sunday after election results started coming in.

item.title: Who Is Peter Magyar, the Man Who Toppled Hungary’s Orban
item.description: Mr. Magyar’s success in the Hungarian election is fueled in large part by widespread public anger at corruption and concern about sluggish economic growth.
item.dc:creator: Aurelien Breeden
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter Magyar at a rally last month for his party, Tisza, in Keszthely, Hungary. He has promised to improve relations with the European Union.

item.title: This Hungarian Town Explains Why Orban Could Lose on Sunday
item.description: Lake Balaton was beloved as a vacation spot. Now, luxury real estate projects serve many friends of Prime Minister Viktor Orban, and the locals are fed up.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter Magyar, who leads the opposition Tisza party, at a rally in Keszthely last month.

item.title: China’s Electrostate Is Poised to Win From War in the Middle East
item.description: A cluster of Chinese firms is poised to win big from the energy crisis set off by the war in the Middle East, building on booming investment in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Keith Bradsher
item.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié for The New York Times
item.media:description: An electricity transmission hub is at the terminus in south-central China of the world’s longest and most powerful ultrahigh-voltage power line. Chinese companies dominate the manufacturing of nearly every piece of a modern grid.

item.title: Trump Says Gas Prices Might Not Drop By Midterms, Highlighting G.O.P. Peril
item.description: The president has sent mixed messages on how long he expects costs to be high. Some in his party voiced fresh concern after his comments on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nathan Howard for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump told Fox News that gas prices might be “a little higher” by the November midterms.

item.title: Iran’s Top Negotiator Says Talks Failed Because U.S. Failed to Win Trust
item.description: Iranian officials said that while Iran had approached the negotiations in good faith, the U.S. team had not gained the Iranian delegation’s trust.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Saturday. Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, Iran’s top negotiator and the speaker of its Parliament, said the U.S. team had failed to “gain the trust of the Iranian delegation.”

item.title: Eric Swalwell Suspends Campaign for California Governor After Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: In a social media post, the representative said he was “deeply sorry for mistakes in judgment I’ve made in my past,” but promised to fight what he called “false allegations.”
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell of California had been in a crowded field vying to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

item.title: California Democrats Rushed to Back Eric Swalwell — and Quickly Abandoned Him
item.description: Representative Eric Swalwell, a Washington figure, was largely unknown in the California State Capitol. But influential groups began backing him for governor in the weeks before he was publicly accused of sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Eric Swalwell is facing calls to resign after he was publicly accused of sexual assault and misconduct.

item.title: ‘The Strangest Sleepover Ever’: Dutch Skeptical as Their Royals Visit Trump
item.description: The Dutch king and queen are set to stay at the White House on Monday night. At a time of trans-Atlantic tension, the Netherlands isn’t sure how it feels about that.
item.dc:creator: Koba Ryckewaert and Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nicolas Tucat/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands are headed to Washington this week to meet with President Trump.

item.title: Trump Fires Board That Oversees Presidio in San Francisco
item.description: All six board members that oversee the national historic landmark were terminated, part of the administration’s efforts to reshape the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Jim Wilson/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has fired the board that oversees the Presidio, a national historic site at the base of the Golden Gate Bridge.

item.title: Cuba’s President Projects Defiance in Standoff With the U.S.
item.description: Miguel Díaz-Canel’s stance on “Meet the Press” reflected how Cuba is digging in against pressure from a Trump administration distracted by the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Adam B. Ellick and Simon Romero
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Adalberto Roque
item.media:description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel of Cuba in Havana last month. He told “Meet the Press” on Sunday, “We’ll defend ourselves, and if we need to die, we’ll die.”

item.title: Pope Leo Heads to Africa to Meet the Future of Catholicism
item.description: On a four-country tour of the continent, where the faith is growing faster than anywhere else, the pontiff will be greeted by enthusiastic crowds and authoritarian leaders.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV in November in Istanbul. On that visit, the pope engaged in interfaith dialogue, and similar discussions may come up during his Africa trip.

item.title: Trump Attacks Pope Leo as Too Liberal and ‘Weak on Crime’
item.description: President Trump’s lengthy post on social media showed there were really no boundaries when it comes to people he might target, including the leader of the world’s 1.4 billion Catholics.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking to the news media at Joint Base Andrews on Sunday night.

item.title: What to Know About Pope Leo’s Voyage to Africa
item.description: The pontiff will visit four countries on the world’s poorest continent, demonstrating his urgent focus on the most vulnerable members of the Church.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:credit: Andrew Medichini/Associated Press
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican last month. His 10-day trip to Africa begins on Monday and includes visits to Algeria, Angola, Cameroon and Equatorial Guinea.

item.title: In Myanmar, Even Flowers Stoke Fear
item.description: Myanmar’s generals have brutalized the country for decades. But they have come to dread garlands as a sign of resistance.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Daniel Berehulak
item.media:description: The bombs come at any time. The only constant is that they will.

item.title: Mamdani Returns to the Stage to Tell the Story of His First 100 Days
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani delivered his 100-day address at a concert venue in Queens. He highlighted his accomplishments and was joined by a not-so-surprising guest.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Olivia Bensimon
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: “With what we’ve accomplished in 14 weeks, imagine what we can do together in four years,” Mayor Zohran Mamdani said.

item.title: Mamdani Plans to Open City-Owned Grocery Store in East Harlem
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced plans for a city-owned grocery store at La Marqueta, moving to deliver on a campaign pledge.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: La Marqueta, a marketplace under elevated train tracks, once covered five city blocks and had many vendors, but now hosts just a few.

item.title: How 6 People in New York City Are Coping With Higher Costs
item.description: Surging inflation and rising prices have some people re-evaluating date nights and how much of their splurges they can share with friends.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy and Vincent Alban
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times; Claire Fahy/The New York Times
item.media:description: People in New York City said they were cutting back in different ways as higher costs for essentials and fun increase at the same time.

item.title: Rejected by Dozens of Emergency Rooms: South Korea’s Medical Crisis
item.description: Hundreds of patients every year have trouble finding emergency care, even though the country has a world-class medical system. For some, the delays have been fatal.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency workers at a hospital in Seongnam, a city outside Seoul.

item.title: Aiming at China, Malaysia Puts New Restrictions on Electric Cars
item.description: The move was a tacit acknowledgment of China’s dominance in the global market and the power of its automakers to keep prices low.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed
item.media:credit: Mohd Rasfan/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An electric car made by Perodua, a Malaysian automaker, at a launch event in Kuala Lumpur in December.

item.title: How I Found Out I Knew a Serial Killer
item.description: A Times reporter met the Gilgo Beach murderer through her co-op. Not long after, the ascendancy of prestige true crime narrative began.
item.dc:creator: Ginia Bellafante
item.category: Lost Girls: An Unsolved American Mystery (Book)
item.media:credit: Johnny Milano for The New York Times
item.media:description: After more than a decade with no suspect in the Gilgo Beach killings, a Long Island architect, Rex Heuermann, was arrested in 2023.

item.title: Shannan Gilbert’s Death Remains the Great Unsolved Mystery of the Gilgo Beach Murders
item.description: The death of 23-year-old Shannan Gilbert remains the great unsolved mystery surrounding the Gilgo Beach serial killer case.
item.dc:creator: Robert Kolker
item.media:credit: Kevin Hagen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mari Gilbert at the burial of her daughter Shannan Gilbert. Shannan’s disappearance led to the investigation of a serial killer; yet her death was never considered a murder.

item.title: Shaping the New Look of ‘Euphoria’
item.description: Natasha Newman-Thomas has stepped in as the new costume designer for the third season of HBO’s chaotic megahit. It’s a lofty task.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: ‘Euphoria’ Creator Sam Levinson Talks Tragedy, the Internet and the End of the Show
item.description: Ahead of the Season 3 premiere on Sunday, the show’s creator discussed his reputation, the loss of Angus Cloud and the soul-searching that followed.
item.dc:creator: Joe Coscarelli and Chantal Anderson
item.media:credit: Chantal Anderson for The New York Times

item.title: The Relationship Between America and the U.A.E. Needs an Upgrade
item.description: For decades, the U.S.-Gulf relationship was based on oil for security. That framework is obsolete.
item.dc:creator: Badr Jafar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump’s War With Iran Has Weakened America
item.description: The president should at long last recognize the ineptitude of his impulsive, go-it-alone approach.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Jason Hendardy for The New York Times

item.title: After Chaotic Election Day, Peru Orders 2nd Day of Voting for Some Areas
item.description: Frustrated voters in long lines were unable to cast ballots in parts of the capital, Lima, due to logistical failures, eroding trust for some in the process.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky and Mitra Taj
item.media:credit: Sara Wayra for The New York Times
item.media:description: A presidential campaign event for Rafael López Aliaga, leader of the Popular Renewal Party, in Lima last Thursday.

item.title: Elon Musk, Who Owns X, Appears to Post on TikTok
item.description: A verified account with the @elonmusk handle also recently showed up on Instagram, as the billionaire prepares to take his rocket company SpaceX public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:credit: Denis Balibouse/Reuters
item.media:description: Elon Musk has previously shown disdain for other social media companies that X competes against.

item.title: Pearl Fryar, a Picasso of Plants, Dies at 86
item.description: A self-taught topiary artist, he discovered a talent for carving trees and shrubs into extraordinary shapes, creating a world-famous garden in a tiny South Carolina town.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green
item.media:credit: Imke Lass for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mr. Fryar in 2005. He began landscaping his yard more than four decades ago in an effort to win an award from the local garden club.
